steps.title: Block the body (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a rounded shape like a fat half-moon — flat across the top, curving down and out at the sides and bottom. This is the basket body seen from the front.

steps.title: Block the lid (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a shallow curved shape sitting on top of the body — slightly wider than the top edge so it overhangs a little. A small rectangular slot cut into the front of the lid is where fish go in.

steps.title: Mark the strap (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a long thin loop rising from one side of the lid, arcing over, and coming down to the other side — the carrying strap. Keep it loose; you'll refine it in ink.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the outline of the body, the lid, and the slot in one mostly continuous pass. Land lines cleanly where the lid meets the body.

steps.title: Ink the strap (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the strap as two parallel curves, meeting the lid cleanly on each side. A small buckle mark partway along adds character.

steps.title: Suggest the weave (ink)
steps.description: Add a scatter of short crosshatch marks across the body to suggest the wicker weave — three or four small clusters is enough. Resist filling every inch; the eye reads the pattern from a few marks.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove pencil guides that distract. The creel should feel handmade, not machine-perfect.
